heading: Financial assistance for Head Start programs
label: 9833 Financial assistance for Head Start programs
text: The Secretary may, upon application by an agency which is eligible for designation as a Head Start agency pursuant to , provide financial assistance to such agency for a period of 5 years for the planning, conduct, administration, and evaluation of a Head Start program focused primarily upon children from low-income families who have not reached the age of compulsory school attendance which (1) will provide such comprehensive health, education, parental involvement, nutritional, social, and other services as will enable the children to attain their full potential and attain school readiness; and (2) will provide for direct participation of the parents of such children in the development, conduct, and overall program direction at the local level.section 9836 of this title
notes: Amendments2007— inserted “for a period of 5 years” after “provide financial assistance to such agency”.Pub. L. 110–1341998—Cl. (1). substituted “enable the children” for “aid the children” and inserted “and attain school readiness” before the semicolon.Pub. L. 105–2851994— struck out subsec. (a) designation, in cl. (1), substituted “health, education, parental involvement, nutritional, social, and other services” for “health, nutritional, educational, social, and other services”, and struck out subsec. (b) which read as follows: “For purposes of providing financial assistance under subsection (a) of this section to agencies, the Secretary may not take into consideration whether such agency applies for or receives funds under subchapter V of this chapter.”Pub. L. 103–2521988— designated existing provisions as subsec. (a) and added subsec. (b).Pub. L. 100–297

Effective Date of 1994 AmendmentAmendment by effective , but not applicable to Head Start agencies and other recipients of financial assistance under the Head Start Act ( et seq.) until , see , set out as a note under .Pub.
